objective: Plan the first narrow implementation child under issue #612, bounded to execution-scope enforcement for degraded same-family fallback fields only.
scope_boundary: Planning-only governance child lane for issue #625.
Owns execution-scope schema/assertion/test enforcement planning only.
Keeps fallback-log schema/workflow parity, packet-routing enforcement, #607 planning-authority work, and #614 downstream verifier work out of scope.
Keeps the dirty primary checkout isolated by working only inside the issue-625 worktree.
out_of_scope: Fallback-log schema or workflow parity surfaces.
Issue #614 downstream verifier or drift-gate work.
research_summary: Grounding intake was completed from docs/PROGRESS.md, issue #612, issue #610, parent issue #615, and the current execution-scope validator surfaces. Specialist review concluded that issue #612 is still too wide for safe implementation as one lane. The first narrow child should therefore own only docs/schemas/execution-scope.schema.json, scripts/overlord/assert_execution_scope.py, and scripts/overlord/test_assert_execution_scope.py, with later #612 children left to handle fallback-log schema/workflow parity and broader review-artifact or packet-routing enforcement.

sprints.name: Intake And Ownership Boundary
sprints.goal: Define why issue #625 exists and keep it narrower than issue #612.
tasks: Review docs/PROGRESS.md, issue #612, issue #610, and the execution-scope validator surfaces.
Record the exact owned execution-scope surface and the explicit out-of-scope fallback-log, packet-routing, #607, and #614 surfaces.
deliverables: Review docs/PROGRESS.md, issue #612, issue #610, and the execution-scope validator surfaces.
State the exact owned files and the explicit out-of-scope fallback-log, packet-routing, #607, and #614 surfaces.
acceptance_criteria: The packet states that issue #625 is the first narrow implementation child under issue #612.
The packet names only docs/schemas/execution-scope.schema.json, scripts/overlord/assert_execution_scope.py, and scripts/overlord/test_assert_execution_scope.py as the owned implementation surface.
The packet explicitly excludes fallback-log schema/workflow parity and broader validator or packet-routing work.

sprints.name: Execution-Scope Proof Contract
sprints.goal: Define the fixed degraded-fallback fields and the exact positive and negative proof cases for the later implementation lane.
tasks: Name the three fixed degraded-fallback fields that later execution-scope enforcement must require.
List the exact positive and negative fail-closed proof cases for the later implementation lane.
Require regression proof that unchanged valid paths still pass.
deliverables: Define the fixed fields cross_family_path_unavailable, cross_family_path_ref, and fallback_log_ref.
Define the positive pass case and the negative fail-closed cases for missing, blank, generic, non-local, and non-existent artifact references.
Require regression proof for ordinary cross-family or non-degraded paths and for existing same-family exception checks.
acceptance_criteria: The packet requires the three fixed degraded-fallback fields by name.
The packet names exact positive and negative proof cases for the later implementation lane.
The packet requires regression proof that existing valid paths still pass unchanged.

sprints.name: Cross-Family Review And Packet Closeout
sprints.goal: Run the sanctioned alternate-family review and close the planning packet cleanly.
tasks: Run the sanctioned Claude review wrapper on the issue-625 packet.
Capture the issue-scoped review artifact and fold any follow-up into the packet.
Run the planning validators and diff hygiene.
deliverables: Hand the issue-625 packet to the sanctioned Claude review wrapper path in this worktree.
Record the issue-scoped alternate-family review artifact and fold any follow-up into the packet.
Run the planning validators and issue-local diff hygiene.
acceptance_criteria: A real alternate-family review artifact exists for issue #625.
The packet validates structurally and remains planning-only.
Implementation remains blocked pending a later implementation lane.

specialist_reviews.focus: Verify bounded ownership, required proof cases, and explicit out-of-scope surfaces for issue #625.
specialist_reviews.summary: Issue #625 should authorize only docs/schemas/execution-scope.schema.json, scripts/overlord/assert_execution_scope.py, scripts/overlord/test_assert_execution_scope.py, and issue-local artifacts, while excluding fallback-log parity, packet-routing enforcement, #607, and #614.

specialist_reviews.focus: Verify the minimum planning packet, handoff, execution-scope fields, and validators required before issue #625 implementation can start.
specialist_reviews.summary: The planning packet needs the canonical structured plan, planning execution scope, accepted handoff, issue/branch alignment, and the standard packet validators before implementation can start.

alternate_model_review.summary: Sanctioned alternate-family review approved the issue-625 planning packet with non-blocking follow-up to refresh validation and cross-review wording on the finalized packet.

execution_handoff.approved_scope_summary: Issue #625 may implement the bounded execution-scope degraded same-family fallback enforcement slice on docs/schemas/execution-scope.schema.json, scripts/overlord/assert_execution_scope.py, scripts/overlord/test_assert_execution_scope.py, required governance doc co-staging, and issue-local evidence artifacts only.
execution_handoff.next_execution_step: Finish implementation-phase validation, closeout, and publish flow for the bounded execution-scope-only child without widening into fallback-log parity or broader #612 enforcement.
handoff_acceptance_criteria: Issue #625 remains bounded to the execution-scope enforcement surface only.
Same-family degraded fallback requires cross_family_path_unavailable == true plus repo-safe existing cross_family_path_ref and fallback_log_ref.
Focused execution-scope proof covers the positive, negative, and regression classes claimed by the issue-local validation artifact.
Issue #625 does not claim #612 closure by itself.

material_deviation_rules: Any patch to schemas, validators, tests, or workflows in issue #625 is a material deviation from planning-only scope.
Any edit to fallback-log schema/workflow parity surfaces is a material deviation from issue #625 scope.
Any edit to packet-routing or broader handoff enforcement surfaces is a material deviation from issue #625 scope.
Any claim that issue #625 closes issue #612 by itself is a material deviation.
Any future closure claim for issue #625 that cites only packet validators instead of execution-scope enforcement tests is a material deviation.
